p1_string_of_nickels.physical_facts: Stable leaf count; foliage remains turgid and oriented toward the light source.
p1_string_of_nickels.explanatory_transformations: Maintained consistent posture throughout the 5-day sequence; no signs of wilting or chlorosis.
p1_string_of_nickels.visual_health_inference: Healthy. The plant shows high resilience to the current VPD environment.

p2_mexican_mint.physical_facts: Two primary leaves present; slight drooping observed in the central growth point.
p2_mexican_mint.explanatory_transformations: The leaf margins have stabilized since the T-4 intervention; no further necrosis progression noted.
p2_mexican_mint.visual_health_inference: Recovering. The stabilization of leaf margins suggests the recent care (starch water) is mitigating previous stress.

p3_pothos.physical_facts: Two leaves present; white rabbit scale anchor (5cm) remains in position.
p3_pothos.explanatory_transformations: Tip necrosis on the larger leaf has remained static since T-5; no expansion of lesions.
p3_pothos.visual_health_inference: Stable. The plant is holding its current state despite the VPD stress.

p4_silver_guest.physical_facts: Small sprout near the rim of the p2/p4 shared pot.
p4_silver_guest.explanatory_transformations: The sprout has remained consistent in size and color throughout the observation period.
p4_silver_guest.visual_health_inference: Stable. Growth is slow but consistent.

biome_observations.soil_surface: Visible white residue (starch water) present on the soil surface of p2/p4, confirming successful application of user care.
biome_observations.incidental_growth: No new weeds or uncatalogued sprouts detected.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: None. Soil moisture appears consistent with the recent starch water application.

temporal_deltas.T_minus_5_to_T_minus_1: Progressive accumulation of starch water residue on soil surface; stabilization of leaf margins on p2.
temporal_deltas.current_state: Soil surface remains moist; plants show no signs of acute distress following the 10:30 AM intervention.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The garden is in a state of 'managed stability'. The starch water application has been successfully integrated into the biome without causing adverse reactions or fungal blooms.
vision_report.anomalies: None. All visual changes are accounted for by the human action log.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit confirms that the plants are responding positively to the recent starch water application. The p2 Mexican Mint, previously flagged for stress, shows no further degradation, indicating that the intervention is effectively supporting its recovery. The p1 String of Nickels and p3 Pothos remain stable, demonstrating resilience to the ongoing VPD stress. The biome is clean, and the scale anchor (rabbit) remains undisturbed.
